description:
Goal
Replace the split PreparedExecutionGraph and PreparedNativeProgram handoff model with one authoritative compiler::World that is passed as mutable compiler state through the spine. The world should accumulate frontend, execution-graph, and native-preparation facts in one place instead of bouncing staged structs through return values.

Signal
- compiler::World subsumes the state currently carried by PreparedExecutionGraph and PreparedNativeProgram.
- Compiler entrypoints mutate a supplied World instead of returning staged prep structs.
- Codegen consumes compiler-owned world state rather than owning a separate prepared-native package.
- CLI, REPL, tests, and docs use the new world boundary and the targeted pipeline suite passes.

Strategy
1. Define the world shape and move execution-graph state into compiler-owned state transitions.
2. Move native-preparation state into the same world and make backend emission consume it.
3. Migrate callers, tests, and docs to the mutable world API and remove the superseded prep structs.

References
- src/compiler.rs
- src/modules/pipeline.rs
- src/ir_codegen/driver.rs
- src/lib.rs
- src/cli/repl.rs
- .agent/docs/single-authoritative-plan.md
title: Epic: make compiler::World the authoritative compiler spine
